evals.name: new-skill-with-evals
evals.prompt: Create a reusable skill named csv-normalizer for converting messy CSV exports into normalized UTF-8 CSV files. Include concise SKILL.md instructions, narrowly scoped script guidance if deterministic cleanup is useful, and objective evals for at least two realistic CSV cleanup cases. Keep long details out of SKILL.md unless they are needed after the skill loads.
evals.expected_output: A compliant csv-normalizer skill directory is created with lean SKILL.md instructions and evals/evals.json covering objective CSV normalization behavior.
expectations: Creates csv-normalizer/SKILL.md with name and description frontmatter, and the name matches the directory
Keeps the SKILL.md body focused on workflow and constraints instead of repeating the frontmatter description
Adds evals/evals.json with at least two realistic objective eval cases
Uses references/ or scripts/ for long details or deterministic helpers when needed
Does not add broad tool approvals or machine-specific paths

evals.name: revise-redundant-skill
evals.prompt: Revise an existing skill whose SKILL.md repeats the description in the opening paragraph, has separate overlapping sections named 'When to use', 'Use this for', and 'Trigger examples', and has no evals. Remove repeated body content while preserving behavior, then add objective evals that cover the skill's main workflow and at least one near-miss trigger case.
evals.expected_output: The revised skill has a lean SKILL.md without overlapping sections and includes evals that guard the main behavior and trigger boundaries.
expectations: Removes or merges overlapping usage sections instead of preserving duplicated headings
Does not copy the description frontmatter into the skill body
Preserves the practical workflow and constraints needed after the skill loads
Adds evals/evals.json with objective success criteria
Includes at least one expectation that would catch accidental trigger-boundary regression

evals.name: package-and-validate-skill
evals.prompt: Prepare an existing skill for distribution after a small instruction update. Validate the skill, package it into a temporary dist directory, and explain where the archive was written. Do not package root evals or cache files.
evals.expected_output: The skill is validated, packaged into a .skill archive, and the final answer reports the archive path without including evals or cache files.
expectations: Runs quick_validate.py against the skill directory
Runs package_skill.py with an explicit output directory
Creates a .skill archive in the requested or temporary distribution directory
Confirms root evals, __pycache__, node_modules, .DS_Store, and .pyc files are excluded
